title: 10 best herbal creams for queasy stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain active compounds like gingerol and shogaol that work to soothe and calm the digestive system. These natural ingredients help to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the production of serotonin, a chemical that can cause stomach upset. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can further contribute to queasiness. As a result, using herbal ginger creams can provide quick and effective relief from queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger's digestive stimulant property helps to increase saliva production, which breaks down food and reduces nausea and vomiting by promoting digestion.

Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and queasiness.

Gastrokinetic Agent: The bioactive compounds in herbal ginger creams, such as gingerols and shogaols, work as gastrokinetic agents, helping to stimulate muscle contractions in the stomach that move food through the digestive system more efficiently, reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate nausea.
Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes contain a bioactive compound called gingerol, which has natural anti-emetic properties that can help reduce vomiting and queasiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh ginger roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Boil the cleaned ginger in 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the boiling mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, retaining the liquid.
Mix the retained liquid with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of shea butter in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture slowly over low flame for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural soothing properties that ease digestive discomfort. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate nausea. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory effects help to calm irritation in the gut, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced symptoms of queasiness. As a result, herbal peppermint creams provide fast and effective relief from upset tummies, making them a popular remedy for soothing stomach woes.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling effect helps to calm digestive discomfort, reducing nausea and stomach spasms by blocking pain receptors in the gut.
Eugenol: Eugenol's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to queasiness and discomfort.
Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint cream, including menthone and limonene, have natural anti-emetic properties that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol and other compounds that help to soothe digestive issues, making them a popular choice for calming an upset stomach.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce nausea and alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for six hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with three tablespoons of coconut oil and add the cooled peppermint infusion to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with queasy stomach because of its calming and soothing properties. The sweet, floral aroma of lavender has a profound effect on digestion, relaxing the muscles in the abdominal area and reducing inflammation. When applied topically as a cream, it can help to calm nausea and vomiting by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce symptoms of queasiness, providing relief from stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea and queasiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can exacerbate symptoms of a queasy stomach such as bloating, gas, and discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The calming effects of lavender can help to alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers for nausea and queasiness, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Seeds: Containing volatile oils, seeds provide a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect on the stomach, alleviating symptoms of queasiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from your garden or purchase high-quality dried lavender flowers.
Measure out 50g of dried lavender flowers and steep them in 200ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Combine 100g of beeswax pellets with 150ml of the infused lavender liquid and heat gently until melted and mixed well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for several hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's gentle, calming effect can ease spasms and cramps in the stomach, reducing nausea and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties help to kill off any unwanted bacteria or viruses that may be contributing to queasiness. Overall, using chamomile creams can provide fast-acting relief from queasy stomach symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile can help to relax muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to queasy feelings.

Calming and soothing flavonoids: Chamomile creams contain flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-related digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used to create a calming and soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate nausea and indigestion.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and relax the stomach muscles, providing relief from queasiness.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in carrier oil by mixing one part of dried flowers with three parts of oil for four hours.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids after two minutes.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil at a ratio of 1:1:1 adding vitamin e oil as preservative in five minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 20-25 minutes stirring occasionally.
Pour the melted cream into sterilized containers allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the digestive system. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work to soothe the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of nausea and indigestion. Additionally, fennel creams can also help to relax the smooth muscle contractions that cause cramping and discomfort, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for individuals experiencing queasy stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream can help relieve muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with queasy stomachs.
Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative effects help to expel gas from the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort caused by digestive issues like indigestion and nausea.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to queasy stomach symptoms such as stomach pain, bloating, and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have a calming effect on digestive issues, which makes them suitable for relieving queasiness and nausea.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are known to soothe stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from queasy stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry foeniculum vulgare seeds in the morning, ensuring complete desiccation within 2-3 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 20 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating gently for 1 hour at 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids after 30 minutes and allowing the liquid to cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled herbal infused oil with 2 grams of beeswax and 0.5 grams of vitamin e oil using an electric mixer until fully incorporated and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm digestive issues. The cream's rich content of dandelion root extract contains prebiotic fibers that promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, regulating digestion and reducing discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate appetite and relieve nausea, making it a gentle yet effective remedy for queasy stomachs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, can soothe and calm digestive inflammation, reducing nausea and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Digestive stimulant: The inulin and fructooligosaccharides present in dandelion cream can stimulate digestion, improve gut motility, and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating often experienced during queasiness.
Antioxidant: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to nausea and stomach upset, promoting overall digestive health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, dandelion roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Fruits (Achenes): The achenes contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes gut health and reduces symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried taraxacum officinale flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler heated at low temperature for 30 minutes.
Add 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated over 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until smooth and creamy, taking about 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive system. The creams' active ingredients, such as martricaria and adiantum, have a calming effect on the gut, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by nausea and stomach upset. By applying these creams topically to the abdomen, individuals can experience relief from queasy stomach symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation in the digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea.

Gastroprotective properties: Ginkgo biloba has been found to possess gastroprotective effects by reducing the production of acid in the stomach and increasing mucus production, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots have a soothing effect on the digestive system, making them an effective ingredient in creams for alleviating queasy stomach.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to calm nausea and indigestion, making it a useful addition to medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in autumn when they are ripe for drying or purchase dried leaves from a reputable supplier.
Combine 200g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 400ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow infusion and then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 20g of vitamin e oil and slowly melt in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl for about 30 seconds at a time.
Combine the infused ginkgo biloba mixture with the melted wax and vitamin e oil and whip until thickened to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with queasy stomach because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues. The active compound curcumin, found in turmeric, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that target the gut, calming irritations and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to repair and regenerate damaged tissues in the stomach lining, promoting a healthy environment for digestion. By applying topical cream directly to the stomach, turmeric helps to alleviate queasiness and indigestion, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and bloating.
Antioxidants: Herbal turmeric creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to stomach upset and queasiness.
Gingerols and Shogaols: Turmeric also contains gingerol and shogaol compounds, which have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Roots: Contain compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 200 grams of coconut oil and mix it with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of turmeric powder to the mixture and stir until well combined for about 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool until it reaches a consistency of 78°f to 85°f, then whip with an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes.
Fill sterilized containers and refrigerate at 40°f to 45°f for at least 2 hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural soothing properties. The herb valerian has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to ease nausea and reduce symptoms of an upset stomach. When applied topically in cream form, it can provide fast relief from queasiness, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Additionally, valerian creams may also help to regulate digestion, reducing discomfort and bloating associated with indigestion and gas.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian cream's sedative properties can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, which can alleviate queasiness.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which means it can help protect the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, reducing symptoms of queasy stomach and digestive discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic properties can help relieve stomach cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with queasy stomach, providing relief from nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are commonly used to create calming and soothing creams that can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a wealth of active compounds that help reduce nausea and vomiting, making them a key component in medicinal creams for queasy stomach relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried valeriana officinalis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground valeriana powder with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler and heat until it reaches a temperature of 60°c over 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 20ml of the herbal oil with 10g of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted over 5 minutes, then pour the cream into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. When applied topically, it can calm inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, reducing nausea and discomfort. Additionally, licorice creams often include other herbs like peppermint and chamomile, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that further aid in easing queasy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to nausea and queasiness.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antacid and anti-ulcer properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from irritation, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can contribute to queasiness and discomfort after eating or due to other factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making them effective in reducing nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: Contains a number of flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach upset and discomfort.
Barks: Possesses antispasmodic properties that can help relax the digestive system and reduce cramps and spasms associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm ventilated area for 7-10 days or use pre-dried root powder.
Combine 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture at room temperature for 2 hours and then filter the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter paper.
Blend the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a creamy consistency takes approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain the natural herb sage, which has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues. The essential oils in sage have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and discomfort. When applied topically, these oils can ease stomach cramps and spasms, calming the digestive system and promoting a sense of relief from queasiness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal sage creams contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can cause nausea and vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of queasiness and indigestion.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils present in sage, such as camphor and borneol, can help to ease gas and bloating in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and nausea associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and alleviating symptoms of a queasy stomach.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the sage plant contains sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and calm digestive discomfort associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves and mix with 10ml of olive oil in a small bowl.
Combine the herb mixture with 100ml of warm water and let it steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard solids and set aside to cool.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets and 10ml of coconut oil to the cooled liquid, heat in a double boiler until melted.
Remove from heat, whip with a hand blender for 2 minutes until thickened and creamy, pour into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with queasy stomach because they possess natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe digestive issues. The herbal extracts in thyme creams work to calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with queasiness. Additionally, thyme's carvacrol content has been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora. By applying topically, thyme creams can help alleviate stomach discomfort and promote overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Thyme's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can exacerbate nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to queasy stomach symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Digestive stimulant property: Thyme's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion and increase appetite, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with queasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm an upset stomach.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain volatile oils that have natural carminative properties, helping to reduce bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or local market on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 1 cup of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and sift through a mesh screen.
Mix 10 tablespoons of powdered thymus vulgaris with 20 tablespoons of cooled coconut oil in a double boiler, heating slowly over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the infused oil mixture into sterilized glass jars, sealing tightly and allowing to cool at room temperature for at least 2 hours before storing in a dark cupboard.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with queasy stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The creamy texture coats the stomach lining, providing a barrier against irritants that can trigger queasiness. Herbal ingredients in oats creams, such as peppermint and ginger, further help to ease digestion and alleviate nausea. By reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, these creams provide relief from queasy stomach symptoms, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oats contain compounds like avenanthramides, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
Soil-binding properties: Oats are able to bind to toxins and other substances in the gut, helping to remove them from the body and reducing irritation and discomfort associated with queasiness.

Soothing and emollient properties: Oat cream's rich, moisturizing ingredients can help soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort, and providing a calming effect on an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing nausea.
Seeds: Oat seeds contain avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the gut, providing relief from queasy stomach.
Barks: Oat barks have been used for centuries as a natural anti-emetic, helping to alleviate vomiting and nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried avena sativa powder into a clean glass bowl.
Combine measured avena sativa powder with 8 ounces of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches 180°f.
Strain the hot liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bowl.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil with the cooled herbal infusion and whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with queasy stomach because of its unique blend of soothing herbs. The cream's active ingredients, such as passionflower and chamomile, work together to calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of nausea and discomfort. The gentle, non-irritating properties of maypop also help to ease inflammation and cramping in the stomach, providing quick relief from queasiness. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and reduces feelings of unease, making it an effective solution for alleviating stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Reduces anxiety and nervousness, which are common causes of queasiness, by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming the digestive system.
Chamomile essential oil: Soothes the stomach lining and reduces inflammation, helping to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, while also promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Ginger extract: Relieves digestive discomfort by reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach, intestines, and esophagus, making it an effective natural remedy for queasy stomachs, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, may help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Fruits: Contain natural antacids that can help neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves according to traditional methods ensuring quality and quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for four hours or until crispy and brittle textures achieved.
Combine one part dried passiflora incarnata with two parts beeswax and six parts coconut oil in a double boiler for gentle heat processing.
Heat the mixture under constant stirring for approximately thirty minutes or until the waxes are fully melted and incorporated into the herbal infusion.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature before whipping and transferring to an air-tight container for storage and usage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with queasy stomach because its calming properties soothe digestive issues, alleviating symptoms of nausea and discomfort. The cream's natural ingredients work to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to queasiness. Additionally, lemon balm has a gentle, anti-inflammatory effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce swelling and irritation that may be exacerbating queasy feelings. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm the digestive muscles, reducing cramping and spasms in the stomach that can contribute to nausea and queasiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other conditions characterized by queasy stomach feelings.

Gastroprotective properties: Lemon balm has been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, gastritis, and other digestive irritants, reducing the likelihood of discomfort, nausea, and queasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe an upset stomach and alleviate nausea.
Leaves: The leaves contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate queasiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh melissa officinalis leaves and rinse them gently with cold distilled water.
Combine the leaves with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 4 to 6 hours under indirect sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Whip the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with queasy stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm digestive issues. The cream's active ingredient, calendula, contains flavonoids that reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea. Additionally, pot marigold creams' antioxidant-rich properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate queasiness, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm in the digestive system.

Antacid and soothing properties: The cream's mucilage content helps to coat and protect the lining of the stomach and intestines, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux and stomach upset.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and vibrant yellow color.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or using desiccant for faster results.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the ground calendula powder with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2 and let it infuse in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container adding beeswax at a ratio of 10% to create a stable cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with queasy stomach because it contains soothing properties that calm digestive discomfort. The herbaceous ingredients, such as marshmallow root, have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce irritation in the stomach and intestines. This gentle relief allows for easier digestion and absorption of nutrients, alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, marshmallow creams often include natural acids and enzymes that break down food more efficiently, further easing queasy stomach sensations and promoting overall digestive harmony.
remedy_properties: Soothing mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps to coat and soothe irritated digestive tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach.
Antacid and anti-inflammatory effects: The root's saponins have been shown to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn, indigestion, and nausea.
Anti-spasmodic properties: Marshmallow root contains flavonoids that can help relax and calm muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm digestive issues, making it an effective ingredient in treating queasy stomach.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, these barks help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root powder from althaea officinalis plants weighing approximately 2 ounces.
Combine the marshmallow root powder with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil and 4 tablespoons of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture over low heat for approximately 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently, until all ingredients are fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping with a hand mixer or whisk for about 5 minutes.
Fill clean glass jars with the finished cream and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with queasy stomach because it soothes and calms the digestive system. The herb's mucilages, which are a type of soluble fiber, coat the lining of the stomach and small intestine, providing a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation. This helps to alleviate symptoms of nausea and discomfort, promoting a sense of relief and relaxation in the body. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce digestive upset caused by stress, anxiety, or food sensitivities.
remedy_properties: Soothing mucilage: The chief active compound of slippery elm, mucilage, forms a protective barrier on the stomach lining, coating and calming irritated tissues and reducing inflammation, which helps to alleviate queasiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the digestive tract, which can contribute to queasy stomach feelings by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.

Stabilizing gut barrier function: Slippery elm has been shown to improve the integrity of the gut lining, preventing leaks and reducing permeability, which can help alleviate nausea and other symptoms associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and coats the stomach lining, providing relief from nausea and discomfort.
Roots: The roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, calming queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ulmus rubra leaves and stems from the wild or cultivate your own plant.
Combine the ulmus rubra with 200ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil together until fully incorporated then add the infused ulmus rubra liquid and stir until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for queasy stomach are topical remedies that harness the natural healing properties of herbs to soothe digestive discomfort. These creams work by stimulating digestion, reducing inflammation, and calming the nervous system to alleviate queasiness. Examples of herbal creams include peppermint-based creams, ginger-infused balms, and licorice root salves. By using these creams, individuals with queasy stomachs can experience relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and heartburn, allowing them to resume their daily activities with greater ease and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat queasy stomach with herbal creams is by applying a topical cream containing soothing herbs like peppermint, chamomile, or ginger. These natural ingredients calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort. Look for creams that are specifically designed to target digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps. Regular application can help alleviate symptoms and promote overall stomach wellness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to queasy stomach that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of nausea, cramps, and diarrhea. These creams can also be used to treat conditions like inflammatory bowel disease and gastritis.
